description: Combine a short, dominant value proposition with immediate product, workflow, or inventory proof in the first viewport or just below it.
evidence_notes: Railway pairs the hero promise with an overlapping product canvas.
Notion opens with a short workspace promise and immediate product-media follow-up.
PostHog frames the hero as a developer-native product surface.
Airbnb puts the search shell at the center of the first screen.
rules: Place the primary user task or product value in the first screen.
Support the hero with concrete product proof rather than abstract decoration alone.
Expose one dominant proof state at a time so the first scan stays fast.
Keep the proof asset large enough to feel trustworthy and legible.
implementation_notes: Use a centered hero stack with a product shell, search shell, or workflow demo anchored directly below the main copy.
On mobile, preserve the same proof concept but simplify the shell and reduce screenshot density.
Treat screenshots and hero art as the likely main payloads and optimize them first.
avoid_when: The product has no credible visual proof yet.
The proof asset would be unreadable at the target viewport sizes.
The hero becomes visually overloaded by combining too many demo states at once.
